decisionMaking: Stable isolated fractures versus unstable patterns requiring fixation

metadata.description: Open reduction and internal fixation of unstable ankle fracture patterns.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Medial Clear Space Widening

metadata.description: Radiographic widening suggesting deltoid incompetence and mortise instability.

metadata.claim_text: Assuming an isolated lateral malleolus fracture is always stable without assessing medial structures.

metadata.description: Ankle fracture classification based on fibular fracture level relative to syndesmosis.
Degenerative joint disease after ankle fracture injury.

metadata.claim_text: The ankle ring concept links malleoli, syndesmosis, and deltoid function — instability reflects ring disruption.

metadata.description: Malreduced ankle fracture with persistent mortise incongruity.
Fracture involving the ankle mortise and malleolar structures.
Composite mortise ring mental model connecting malleoli, syndesmosis, and deltoid.
Superficial peroneal nerve at risk in lateral ankle surgery.
Ankle joint congruity and stability of the mortise ring.

relationship_metadata.note: biomechanics_concept linked via involves_anatomy until dedicated predicate exists

metadata.description: Transyndesmotic fibular fracture at syndesmosis level.
Operative fixation method for unstable ankle fractures.
Inadequate restoration of syndesmotic alignment after fixation.

metadata.action: Proceed with operative fixation pathway (ORIF) including syndesmotic assessment/fixation when indicated
metadata.trigger: Unstable mortise pattern (e.g., medial clear space widening, bimalleolar injury, failed closed reduction)

metadata.claim_text: Ankle fracture management hinges on mortise congruity, skin status, neurovascular exam, and syndesmotic integrity.
Medial clear space widening suggests deltoid incompetence and an unstable ankle fracture pattern.

metadata.description: Talar dome and talar body at the ankle joint.

metadata.claim_text: A deltoid ligament injury can make an isolated fibular fracture functionally unstable (bimalleolar equivalent).
Stress views may help evaluate medial clear space and syndesmotic injury when plain films are equivocal.

metadata.action: Nonoperative immobilization with protected weight-bearing plan and repeat radiographs
metadata.trigger: Stable mortise with acceptable alignment after reduction

metadata.claim_text: On rounds, state whether the mortise is stable, comment on syndesmotic injury, and outline splint and weight-bearing plan.
